unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: Welcome to your last unit of Year 2 English! In this unit you will explore a busy city. You will learn the names of city places like the library, the shopping center and the Ferris wheel. You will visit an aquarium and describe amazing sea animals such as the octopus, the penguin, the turtle and the shark. You will say what you like and don't like, give simple directions to help people get around, and talk about things you did and things you will do. By the end, you will be ready to explore your own city, just like Samira and Ishmael do in this unit's story.
unit.learningPath:
Watch the teacher audiovisual lecture and learn a few vocabulary words each day.
Complete all six grammar practices, moving from guided examples to independent use.
Listen to the Unit readings, including A Big Day in the City, and answer the comprehension questions.
Complete all six speaking, six writing and six activity practices.
Join three live teacher sessions each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question quiz and student self-assessment.

visual.alt: Children exploring city places, transport and an aquarium

readings.title: A Poem: “At the Zebra Crossing”
readings.passageScript:
A zebra crossing is a safe place to cross the road. Read this poem:
At the zebra crossing,
Look around at city places.
Look around at city faces.
I bend down to tie my laces… Oops!
WALK! Hurry up! Let's cross!

readings.title: The Aquarium Schedule
readings.passageScript:
Read this schedule from the city aquarium:
Meet our amazing animals!
10:00 — Octopus Show (They're clever!)
11:00 — Penguin Feeding (They're beautiful!)
12:00 — Shark Feeding (They're hungry!)
2:00 — Turtle Time (They're huge!)

readings.title: The City from the Sky
readings.passageScript:
Adam and his dad take a helicopter ride. (Ask your AI tutor to read it aloud, then read it yourself.)
“Pilot: From up here, you can see the whole city! Adam: Wow, look at all the traffic on the roads! Pilot: That tall building is the library. Adam: I love the library! Pilot: And there is the shopping center. Dad: I like shopping. Adam: I don't! Pilot: The ferry takes people across the river. Adam: And look — I can see the Ferris wheel in the park!”

readings.title: A Big Day in the City
readings.theme: Exploring a city, getting around, and helping a stranger who is lost.
readings.setting: A busy town by the sea in East Africa.
readings.passageScript:
On Saturday morning, Samira and her big brother Ishmael took the bus into the city. It was Samira's first big day exploring, and her eyes were wide.
“Look at all the traffic!” said Samira. Cars and buses filled the roads. A helicopter flew high above the tall buildings.
“The city is amazing,” said Ishmael. “Come, I will show you the market first.”
The market was huge and busy. People were buying fish, fruit and bright cloth. Samira smelled fresh bread and sweet mangoes.
“I like the market,” said Samira happily.
“I do too!” said Ishmael. “Now, let's go to the library. It is my favourite place.”
But near the shopping center, they saw an old man. He looked worried. He held a small map and turned it this way and that.
“Excuse me,” the old man said kindly. “I am lost. Can you help me? I need to find the hospital. My daughter is there with a new baby.”
Samira looked at Ishmael. Their mother always said, “A kind person helps a stranger.”
“We can help you,” said Ishmael. “The hospital is not far.”
Isaac pointed down the road. “Walk straight ahead to the traffic light,” he said. “Then turn left. Walk to the next street, and the hospital is on your right. It is a big white building.”
The old man smiled, but his face was still a little worried. “Thank you,” he said. “But my legs are slow, and the traffic is scary.”
“Then we will walk with you,” said Samira.
So the three of them walked together. They crossed the road carefully at the zebra crossing. They walked straight ahead, turned left, and there was the hospital — a big white building, just as Ishmael had said.
“You are here!” said Samira.
“Thank you, my friends,” said the old man. His eyes were bright now. “You are clever and kind children. Today you helped a stranger. May Allah reward you.”
He waved goodbye and went inside to meet his new grandchild.
Samira felt warm inside. “Helping him was better than the market,” she said.
“I do too,” laughed Ishmael. “Come — the library is close. And after that, I have a surprise.”
At the library, Samira read a beautiful book about the sea. She learned that an octopus has eight arms and that a turtle carries a hard shell on its back.
Then came the surprise. Ishmael took her down to the river, and they rode the ferry across the water. From the boat, Samira could see the whole city.
“Ishmael, look!” she cried. “I can see the Ferris wheel!”
The great wheel turned slowly against the blue sky. The city was beautiful.
That night, Samira told her mother everything — the huge market, the lost old man, the library book, and the ferry ride.
“You had a big day,” her mother said, hugging her. “And you helped someone who needed you. That is the best thing of all.”
Samira closed her eyes and smiled. “Tomorrow,” she whispered, “I will explore the city again.”

comprehension.question: What is a zebra crossing? Why is it a safe place?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: A zebra crossing is a safe place to cross the road. At the zebra crossing, Look around at city places.
comprehension.explanation: The response should point to this evidence from the text: A zebra crossing is a safe place to cross the road. At the zebra crossing, Look around at city places.

comprehension.question: What does the person in the poem do before crossing?
comprehension.correctAnswer: The person bends down to tie their laces before crossing.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

grammar.title: Likes and Dislikes
grammar.explanation: In a busy city, there is so much to do! We use like and don't like to say what we enjoy and what we do not enjoy. We can also agree or disagree politely with a friend.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
I like… → something you enjoy:  “I like riding on the ferry.”
I don't like… → something you do not enjoy:  “I don't like the traffic.”
I do too! → to agree:  “I like the library.” — “I do too!”
I don't. → to disagree politely:  “I like shopping.” — “I don't.”
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: “I no like traffic.”  ✗
Fix: We say don't before the verb: “I don't like traffic.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: don't = do not. When you do NOT enjoy something, put don't in front of like: “I don't like…”.
grammar.practice:
Write a “like” or “don't like” answer for each one:
riding on the underground → I ______ riding on the underground.
loud traffic → I ______ loud traffic.
Your friend says: “I like the aquarium.” You agree. You say: “______!”
Your friend says: “I like the Ferris wheel.” You disagree. You say: “______.”

grammar.title: Likes and Dislikes: Use It
grammar.explanation: In a busy city, there is so much to do! We use like and don't like to say what we enjoy and what we do not enjoy. We can also agree or disagree politely with a friend. Now apply the same language independently in the Unit 9 context.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
I like… → something you enjoy:  “I like riding on the ferry.”
I don't like… → something you do not enjoy:  “I don't like the traffic.”
I do too! → to agree:  “I like the library.” — “I do too!”
I don't. → to disagree politely:  “I like shopping.” — “I don't.”
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: “I no like traffic.”  ✗
Fix: We say don't before the verb: “I don't like traffic.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: don't = do not. When you do NOT enjoy something, put don't in front of like: “I don't like…”.
grammar.practice: Create four new examples that follow this rule. Read each one aloud, check it against the model, and correct one possible mistake.

grammar.title: Present Simple for Facts
grammar.explanation: We use the present simple to tell true facts and things that happen again and again. It is perfect for facts about city places and animals.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
Use the plain verb with: I, you, we, they.
I visit the library every week.
They take the bus to school.
Sharks swim in the sea.
Add -s or -es with: he, she, it (and one animal or place).
She goes to the aquarium on Saturdays.
The library opens at nine o'clock.
An octopus has eight arms.
grammar.commonMistake: Mistake: “The ferry take people across the river.”  ✗    Fix: “The ferry takes people across the river.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: One thing (it, he, she) → add the “s”. Think: “It, he, she — don't forget the s!”
grammar.practice:
Write the correct verb:
The library ______ (open) at nine o'clock.
The ferry ______ (take) people across the river.
Octopuses ______ (have) eight arms.
Sharks ______ (swim) fast in the water.
A penguin ______ (not fly), but it ______ (swim) very well.

grammar.title: Present Simple for Facts: Use It
grammar.explanation: We use the present simple to tell true facts and things that happen again and again. It is perfect for facts about city places and animals. Now apply the same language independently in the Unit 9 context.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
Use the plain verb with: I, you, we, they.
I visit the library every week.
They take the bus to school.
Sharks swim in the sea.
Add -s or -es with: he, she, it (and one animal or place).
She goes to the aquarium on Saturdays.
The library opens at nine o'clock.
An octopus has eight arms.
grammar.commonMistake: Mistake: “The ferry take people across the river.”  ✗    Fix: “The ferry takes people across the river.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: One thing (it, he, she) → add the “s”. Think: “It, he, she — don't forget the s!”
grammar.practice: Create four new examples that follow this rule. Read each one aloud, check it against the model, and correct one possible mistake.

grammar.title: The Past and the Future
grammar.explanation: When we explore the city, we like to say what we DID yesterday and what we WILL do tomorrow. Two little time words help us: the past and the future.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
For the past (already happened), we often change the verb:
Today we go → Yesterday we went to the market.
We eat → We ate at the café.
We see → We saw the sharks.
For the future (has not happened yet), we use will + the plain verb:
Tomorrow we will ride the Ferris wheel.
Tomorrow I will visit the library.
grammar.commonMistake: Mistake: “Tomorrow we will rode the ferry.”  ✗    Fix: after will, use the plain verb: “Tomorrow we will ride the ferry.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: will points forward to the future. After will, keep the verb simple: will go, will see, will ride.
grammar.practice:
Write past or future, following the word in brackets:
Yesterday we ______ (go, past) to the aquarium.
Tomorrow I ______ (visit, future) the library.
We ______ (eat, past) fruit at the market.
Tomorrow they ______ (ride, future) the ferry.

grammar.title: The Past and the Future: Use It
grammar.explanation: When we explore the city, we like to say what we DID yesterday and what we WILL do tomorrow. Two little time words help us: the past and the future. Now apply the same language independently in the Unit 9 context.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
For the past (already happened), we often change the verb:
Today we go → Yesterday we went to the market.
We eat → We ate at the café.
We see → We saw the sharks.
For the future (has not happened yet), we use will + the plain verb:
Tomorrow we will ride the Ferris wheel.
Tomorrow I will visit the library.
grammar.commonMistake: Mistake: “Tomorrow we will rode the ferry.”  ✗    Fix: after will, use the plain verb: “Tomorrow we will ride the ferry.”  ✓
grammar.memoryTip: will points forward to the future. After will, keep the verb simple: will go, will see, will ride.
grammar.practice: Create four new examples that follow this rule. Read each one aloud, check it against the model, and correct one possible mistake.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 — Talk About City Places
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Choose three city places (library, shopping center, ferry, Ferris wheel, aquarium). For each one, say:
“This is a ______.”
“People go there to ______.”
“I like / don't like ______ because ______.”
Record yourself. Check: Did you name the place? Did you say what people do there? Did you speak clearly?

speaking.title: Speaking 2 — I Like / I Don't Like
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Take turns with your AI tutor or your family. One person says one thing they like. The other answers “I do too!” or “I don't.”
“I like riding on the ferry.”  →  “I do too!”
“I like shopping.”  →  “I don't.”

speaking.title: Speaking Game — Mystery Animal
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Choose an aquarium animal but do not say its name. Give clues, and let the other person guess:
“I am thinking of an animal. It is ______ and ______.”
“It can ______. It has ______.”
“What animal am I thinking of?” Then swap turns.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - City Guide
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Give six clear directions from one city place to another.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Use go straight, turn left, turn right, next to and opposite.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Aquarium Expert
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Describe three sea animals and state two true facts about each.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Use precise adjectives and the present simple.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - Yesterday and Tomorrow
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Say three things you did in the city and three things you will do next time.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Keep past and future time words clear.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 1 — Describe a City Place
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Choose one city place. Write 3–4 sentences: What is it? What do people do there? What can you find there? Do you like it? Why?
Example: “The library is a quiet place. People go there to read and borrow books. There are many books and soft chairs. I like the library because I can find good stories.”
writing.modelText: I visited the city with my family. We went to the library and saw the market. My favourite place was the aquarium.
writing.sentenceStarter: I visited the city with my family.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 2 — My City Postcard
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Imagine you spent a day in the city. Write a short postcard to a friend:
“Today I ______.”  (past — what you did)
“Tomorrow I will ______.”  (future — what you will do)
Then draw a picture of your favourite city place on the other side.
writing.modelText:
Dear Selma,
I am having a wonderful day in the city. I visited the library and the aquarium. My favourite place was the aquarium because I saw a huge turtle.
From, Samira
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Story response: A Big Day in the City
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Write three sentences about a place you would like to explore in a city. Start with: “I would like to visit ______.”
Imagine you meet someone who is lost. Write the directions you would give them. Use “straight ahead”, “turn left” and “turn right”.
writing.modelText: Samira and Ishmael explored the city and helped a man find the hospital. Samira discovered that helping someone felt better than visiting a busy place. Kindness made their city day special.
writing.sentenceStarter: Samira and Ishmael explored the city and helped a man find the hospital.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 4 - My City Guide
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write six sentences introducing useful or interesting places in your city or town.
writing.modelText: Our library is near the market. People read and study there. The ferry carries passengers across the water.
writing.sentenceStarter: Our library is near the market
writing.successCriteria: I answered every part; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; checked capitals, spelling and punctuation
writing.support: Use the model, Unit word bank and sentence starters. Say each sentence before writing it.
writing.extension: Add one precise detail and explain why it matters.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 5 - An Aquarium Animal Report
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write six facts about one sea animal and include at least three describing words.
writing.modelText: An octopus is a clever sea animal. It has eight arms and can hide between rocks.
writing.sentenceStarter: An octopus is a clever sea animal
writing.successCriteria: I answered every part; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; checked capitals, spelling and punctuation
writing.support: Use the model, Unit word bank and sentence starters. Say each sentence before writing it.
writing.extension: Add one precise detail and explain why it matters.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 6 - A City Day: Past and Future
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write three past-tense sentences about a city visit and three future sentences about another visit.
writing.modelText: Yesterday, we went to the market. We saw busy traffic. Tomorrow, we will visit the library.
writing.sentenceStarter: Yesterday, we went to the market
writing.successCriteria: I answered every part; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; checked capitals, spelling and punctuation

activities.title: Activity 1 — Fill in the City Place
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Write the correct place on each line.
People read and borrow books at the ______.
The ______ takes people across the river.
People buy clothes at the ______.
The ______ is a train that runs under the city.
You can see the whole city from the top of the ______.
You can see sea animals at the ______.
activities.answerSummary: 1. library  2. ferry  3. shopping center  4. underground  5. Ferris wheel  6. aquarium

activities.title: Activity 2 — True or False (City & Animals)
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Write True or False. If it is false, write the correct answer.
The underground is a train that runs below the city.
Octopuses have six arms.
Penguins can fly high in the sky.
Turtles have a hard shell to protect them.
activities.answerSummary: 1. True  2. False (eight arms)  3. False (they cannot fly, but they swim)  4. True

activities.title: Activity 3 — Match the Place to the Activity
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Draw a line to match. Check with your AI tutor.
Library  →  reading and borrowing books
Shopping center  →  buying clothes and things
Ferry  →  crossing the river
Ferris wheel  →  enjoying a ride with a view
Aquarium  →  looking at sea animals

activities.title: Activity 4 - City Map Route
activities.instructionsAndItems: Follow six direction clues on a city map, then write a route to the library.
activities.answerSummary: Answers vary. Require complete, relevant work that follows every instruction and uses the target Unit language accurately.

quizzes.question: What does 'library' mean?
quizzes.options: A building where books are kept to read or borrow. | A large building with many shops inside. | A train that runs below the city streets. | A boat that carries people across water.
quizzes.correctAnswer: A building where books are kept to read or borrow.
quizzes.explanation: I go to the library to borrow books.

quizzes.question: Which word means: A large building with many shops inside.
quizzes.explanation: We bought new clothes at the shopping center.

quizzes.question: What type of word is 'underground'?
quizzes.explanation: We took the underground to the museum.

quizzes.question: We took the ____ across the river.
quizzes.explanation: We took the ferry across the river.

quizzes.question: Which spelling is correct?
quizzes.explanation: We saw the whole city from the Ferris wheel.

quizzes.question: Which word means: A busy place where people buy and sell things.
quizzes.explanation: market: A busy place where people buy and sell things. Mum buys fruit and fish at the market.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which word means: The many cars and vehicles moving on the roads.
quizzes.explanation: traffic: The many cars and vehicles moving on the roads. There is a lot of traffic in the city center.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: What type of word is 'helicopter'?
quizzes.explanation: helicopter: An aircraft that can fly straight up and hover. We saw the city from a helicopter.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: We take the ____ to school every morning.
quizzes.explanation: bus: A large vehicle that carries many people. We take the bus to school every morning.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which spelling is correct?
quizzes.explanation: map: A drawing that shows where places are. We used a map to find the hospital.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

teacherNotes.note:
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the city poem with me and explain it?” “Can you play a listening game about city places?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me practise talking about city places?” “Can you play the Mystery Animal game with me?” “Can you check if my pronunciation is correct?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check if my sentences are correct?” “How can I make my writing more interesting?”
Story: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the story with me and explain any hard words?” “Can you check my directions and my sentences?” “Can you ask me questions about the story to see if I understood?”
Grammar: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check my grammar answers?” “Why do we say ‘she goes’ but ‘I go’?” “Can you give me five more sentences to practise the past and the future?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “What other important places are in a city?” “What is the difference between a shopping center and a market?” “How are cities in East Africa the same or different from other cities?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me practise giving directions?” “What other ways can people travel around a city?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “What other animals might I see at an aquarium?” “Why are octopuses called clever?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “When should I use each describing word?” “What other adjectives can I use to describe animals and places?”

Term placement approved. Outcome mappings, comprehension guidance, vocabulary enrichment, assessments, live sessions, rubrics, writing specifications and answer-key coverage completed in the approved reference model.
